page: UpCloud PREMIUM-2xCPU-16GB is a memory-optimized virtual server hosted on the KVM hypervisor. It features 2 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled with 150 GB of SSD capacity, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks reveal a stark contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The server achieves top-tier results in single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression tasks, such as gzip and bzip3 decompression. However, multi-threaded performance is more limited, with multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and overall PassMark CPU scores falling into the lower percentiles. This discrepancy is influenced by the shared CPU allocation model, which limits sustained multi-core execution. Memory bandwidth remains average, while database operations and Redis SET throughput perform in the lower tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this server offers a high memory density relative to its vCPU count, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require continuous, heavy multi-core processing. It is well-suited for lightweight databases, caching layers, single-threaded data processing, and basic web serving. It is less suitable for complex multi-threaded computations or large-scale language model inference.
description: UpCloud PREMIUM-2xCPU-16GB is a memory-optimized virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor. It is equipped with 2 shared vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 9575F processor operating at 2.0 GHz, paired with 16.0 GB of RAM and 150 GB of local SSD storage. Benchmark data indicates top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds, though multi-core and database operations fall into lower performance tiers due to the shared CPU allocation. This resource profile offers high memory density per core, making it a cost-effective choice for memory-intensive but compute-light applications. It is best suited for lightweight caching, single-threaded data processing, and basic web serving workloads.
